title: The Autism Spectrum Disorder Strategic Fund - Canada.ca
content:
## The Autism Spectrum Disorder Strategic Fund
This invitation to submit an application is now closed.
Autism is a neurological condition that affects how the brain functions.
 Autism is characterized by challenges in social communication and
 interaction, and by restricted, repetitive patterns of behavior, interests,
 or activities. The way autism affects a person varies. It is a "spectrum"
 disorder because each person with autism is unique. The support needs of
 individuals on the autism spectrum may also range from none to very
 substantial.
Budget 2018 announced $9.1 million over five years for an Autism Spectrum
Disorder Strategic Fund.
The fund is designed to support innovative community-based projects that
 will provide tangible opportunities for Canadians on the autism spectrum,
 their families and caregivers to gain knowledge, resources and skills.
 Project activities should lead to improved health behaviours and, in the
 longer term, improve wellbeing of individuals on the autism spectrum, their
families and caregivers.

## Autism Spectrum Disorder Strategic Fund Call for Proposals
The Public Health Agency of Canada (PHAC) is pleased to invite eligible
 organizations to submit applications for community-based projects that
 provide opportunities for the autism community to gain knowledge, resources
 and skills. Projects should include activities that will improve health
 behaviours, address the negative impacts of the COVID-19 pandemic and
 support the mental, physical and social wellbeing of individuals on the
 autism spectrum, their families and caregivers. The focus of this call for
 proposals is on innovative approaches to meet current or emerging priority
areas for the autism community in the context of the pandemic.

## How to apply to the Autism Spectrum Disorder Strategic Fund
Applications must be submitted using PHAC's Invitation to Submit a Funding
  Request (ISFR) template. To obtain a copy of the template or to obtain more
  information, please send an email to phac.cgc.solicitations-csc.aspc@canada.ca and include "Autism Spectrum Disorder Strategic Fund" in the subject line.
  The deadline to submit an ISFR is 3:00 p.m (EST) April 26, 2021. Please
  note no extensions will be granted.
PHAC is under no obligation to enter into a funding agreement as a
 result of this invitation to submit an ISFR. Having a successful ISFR
is not a commitment on the part of PHAC to fund a full proposal.
PHAC also reserves the right to:
- reject any submissions received in response to this invitation;
- accept any submission in whole or in part; and
- cancel and/or re-issue this invitation to submit an ISFR at any time.
Please note that PHAC will not reimburse an applicant for costs incurred in
 the preparation and/or submission of an ISFR in response to this
invitation.
